content: I can't believe it, they've actually accepted our offer on the flat in Bristol! We've been looking for absolute ages and I'm just over the moon.
The baby took her first steps today right towards the dog. It was the most brilliant thing I've ever seen, I nearly cried happy tears.
Found a tenner in my old winter coat today. Not a massive deal but it certainly made my morning commute a bit brighter.
I just got back from the vet's and we had to put Buster down. The house feels completely empty without his little paws clicking on the floorboards.
My mum's dementia is getting much worse and she didn't recognise me when I visited her today. I just sat in the car and wept afterwards.
Gutted about the football match last night. We played so well but to lose on penalties in the last minute is just proper rubbish.
If that bloke from upstairs plays his bass guitar until 3 AM one more time I am going to lose my mind. I've asked him politely three times now and he just laughs in my face.
I've been waiting for this parcel for two weeks, and today they marked it as 'delivered' when I was sitting right by the window. They didn't even knock, they're absolute cowboys!
Someone's taken my milk from the office fridge again, even though my name is written on it in massive black marker. It's just so petty.
I was walking home through the park and I'm certain someone was following me. I had to run the last stretch to my door and my chest is still tight with panic.
The doctor wants me to go in for a biopsy on Tuesday. I am absolutely terrified of what the results might be, I can't sleep at all.
There is a massive spider sitting right above my bedroom door and I daren't walk under it. I know it's silly but I'm completely stuck here.
Oh my god, my sister just turned up at my door! She was supposed to be in Australia for another six months but she planned this whole secret trip to surprise me.
I opened my results email expecting the worst and I've somehow got a first-class degree! I'm genuinely shocked, I was convinced I'd failed that last module.
Well, that was unexpected. The local Tesco has actually got fresh tomatoes in stock for once.
Just sitting in the garden with a proper cup of tea, listening to the birds. No phone, no emails, just peace and quiet for an hour.
The train journey up to Scotland was lovely. I just stared out at the hills and read my book, feeling completely at ease for the first time in weeks.
Finished the washing up and now I'm just going to potter about the house. Nothing much planned for the rest of Sunday.
I'm supposed to give a presentation to the board tomorrow. I know I'm prepared, but my stomach is in absolute knots thinking about it.
Had a lovely chat with the lady at the bus stop today about her allotment. It's nice when strangers are just genuinely pleasant, isn't it?
The boiler has packed in again and the landlord is ignoring my texts. It's freezing in here and I'm just so tired of dealing with this flat.
We had to cancel the holiday to Spain because of the flight strikes. The kids are devastated and I'm just exhausted trying to get a refund.
I can't believe how much the price of butter has gone up. It's bloody ridiculous, everything in the shops is just getting out of hand.
I just saw a shooting star whilst taking the bins out. Quite a magical little moment, really.
I've decided to quit my job. I don't have anything else lined up yet, which is terrifying, but staying there was destroying my soul.
I honestly can't believe it, Mum! I got the promotion and they're doubling my salary. We're actually going to be able to buy that cottage in Devon!
The vet just called to say there's nothing more they can do for Barney. I'm sitting in the car park completely numb, dreading going home to an empty flat.
They've cancelled the trains again and there's absolutely no replacement bus in sight. I'm going to miss my sister's wedding because of this absolute shambles of a service!
I think someone's trying to force the back door open. I'm hiding upstairs in the dark and I can hear footsteps on the patio right now.
Bloody hell, I just opened the cupboard and a giant spider jumped straight out at me! My heart is hammering against my ribs, I actually shrieked.
Just sitting by the canal with a hot brew, watching the swans drift past. It's so peaceful today with the autumn leaves falling around the path.
It was lovely seeing Grandad today; he seemed really bright and even remembered all our names. We had tea in the garden and chatted about his old sailing days.
I dropped my favourite ceramic mug this morning and it smashed into tiny pieces. It was just a silly souvenir from Cornwall, but it's really ruined my mood.
My flatmate has left his dirty plates in the sink for the third day running. I've asked him politely twice now, but he just ignores it and plays FIFA.
I've got my driving test in an hour and my stomach is in absolute knots. I keep worrying I'm going to stall at the double roundabout near the bypass.
Oh, look at that! I completely forgot I put a tenner in my winter coat pocket last year. That's my lunch sorted for today then.
The presentation went reasonably well and now I'm just sorting through my emails before heading home. Looking forward to a quiet evening on the sofa.
The postman just delivered my parcel a day earlier than expected. Quite pleased with that, actually, as I can set up the new router tonight.
It's a bit of a shame that the local bakery is closing down next month. Their sourdough was always spot on, but I suppose high street rents are too steep now.
They've changed the recipe for these biscuits and they taste incredibly bland now. Why do brands always have to ruin a good thing to save a few pennies?
I hope the weather holds out for the barbecue on Saturday. The forecast says light showers, which would be typical for a bank holiday.
I didn't expect the supermarket to be this busy at ten on a Tuesday morning. The queue for the self-checkout goes halfway down the aisle.
Just folding the laundry while listening to the radio. It's a bit of a chore but it's quite nice having nothing else to rush off and do.
I can't believe she lied straight to my face about where she was last night. After three years together, I thought we at least had basic respect for each other.
Well, blow me down! My old uni mate just randomly turned up at the pub we used to frequent in Bristol. We're currently having a pint and catching up on everything.
The turbulence was pretty terrifying for a minute there, but the pilot's calm voice over the intercom really helped settle my nerves. We're flying smoothly now.
We won! In the very last second of extra time, the ball went straight into the top corner. Everyone in the pub went absolutely mental!
I had to say goodbye to my daughter at Heathrow this morning as she moves to Australia for good. Walking back to the car alone was the hardest thing I've ever done.
The cottage is completely off-grid, so there's no phone signal or internet at all. I've spent the whole afternoon reading by the log burner, feeling entirely relaxed.
The council have dug up the road right outside my shop for the third time this year, completely blocking the entrance. It's killing my foot traffic and nobody will take responsibility!
